papers.title_en: Unprivileged Topology Certificates for Cloud GPU Attestation
papers.evidence: 提出基于延迟拓扑指纹的纯软件GPU证明原语，使云租户能验证物理加速器。
papers.tldr: 云GPU租户无法直接检查运行的物理加速器。本文提出一种无特权软件证明原语，利用CUDA探针测量SM级延迟矩阵并生成离线可验证的证书。实验表明，该延迟图是稳定物理指纹，6小时负载下中位抖动仅0.09周期，跨Blackwell芯片分类准确率达100%。同时，该方法能恢复跨代硬件拓扑，包括V100统一内存域、H200 L2分片、B200双芯片间30周期延迟代价。结合169个RIPE Atlas网络探针，将服务器定位在声称数据中心44公里内，成功拒绝所有11个诱饵站点。该技术无需特权或供应商密钥即可验证云GPU真实性、类别与粗粒度位置。
papers.abstract_en: Cloud GPU tenants receive a model name and a region, but cannot directly inspect the physical accelerator that runs their job. We present a software-only attestation primitive for this setting. A CUDA probe measures an SM-by-memory-region latency matrix using physical SM labels and dependent global loads. A streaming reducer commits sufficient statistics, configuration, code hashes, network evidence, and a compressed raw data archive into a certificate that a verifier can check without a GPU. The certificate supports three claims. First, the per-SM latency map is a stable physical fingerprint. Over a six-hour full-load RTX 5090 run, its median temporal jitter is 0.09 cycles, while shape-only leave-one-out classification separates distinct Blackwell dies with 100.0% accuracy. Second, cache-bypassing HBM sweeps recover hardware-class topology across generations, including a unified Volta V100 memory domain, a two-way Hopper H200 L2 split, and a Blackwell B200 two-die NV-HBI package whose 74/74 SM partition carries a 30-cycle, 15.5 ns cross-die penalty. Third, public network landmarks bind the same certificate to a coarse location. In the B200 run, 169 RIPE Atlas probes place the server within 44 km of its claimed datacentre and reject all 11 decoy sites. Together, these measurements check cloud-GPU identity, class, and coarse location without privileged access or a vendor key.

papers.title_en: Kops: Safely Extending the eBPF Compilation Pipeline with Native Operations
papers.authors: Yusheng Zheng, Zhengjie Ji, Weichen Tao, Hao Sun, Wei Zhang, Dan Williams, Andi Quinn
papers.evidence: 通过原生操作安全扩展eBPF以增强内核防御
papers.tldr: eBPF安全扩展内核但JIT单次编译缺乏优化，性能测度比原生慢两倍。本工作提出Kops扩展接口，允许用户态编译器和内核模块引入新操作，以证明序列通过验证器、以原生发射供JIT编译，TCB仅增加每操作的原生代码，内核核心无需改动。基于此构建的EInsn包含七个硬件习语操作，在x86-64与ARM64上微基准最快加速24%，真实应用加速12%；全程序原生替换更达2.358倍。Kops在最小TCB下显著提升eBPF性能，降低优化部署门槛与安全风险。
papers.abstract_en: eBPF safely extends OS kernels in domains such as networking, observability, and security. The safety comes from an in-kernel compilation pipeline where a verifier checks every program, and a kernel just-in-time compiler (JIT) translates the verified bytecode to native code. The kernel keeps the JIT simple to stay trustworthy, translating one bytecode instruction at a time in a single pass. This single-pass design misses optimization opportunities, so eBPF runs up to twice as slow as natively compiled code in our characterization. Adding optimizations to the kernel JIT directly requires upstream acceptance and a long release cycle, enlarges the trusted computing base (TCB), and grows the per-architecture kernel code. To address this, we present Kops, an extension interface that lets userspace compilers and kernel modules introduce new operations without modifying the kernel core, while keeping a minimal trusted computing base (TCB). Each operation has two forms, a proof sequence of vanilla eBPF instructions that the existing verifier checks and a native emit of machine instructions that the JIT compiles. Because the verifier checks the proof sequence, the native emit is the only per-operation addition to the TCB. Hardware idioms are the lowest-hanging fruit for this interface. With Kops, we build EInsn, seven operations such as rotate and conditional select that CPUs execute as single instructions. Lean 4 proofs show that each native emit computes the same result as its proof sequence. On x86-64 and ARM64, EInsn speeds up eBPF microbenchmarks by up to 24% and production applications by up to 12%. The same interface also supports whole-program native replacement, reaching 2.358x at the cost of a larger TCB.
